title: CAWS Event (envelope)
description: Common envelope for every event in .caws/events.jsonl. Per-event payload schemas live under events/<event>.v1.json. Events are append-only; the kernel hash chain is computed over canonicalJson(event minus event_hash) with domain separator 'caws.events.v1' + NUL.

seq.description: Monotonic 1-based sequence. Authority for event ordering (NOT ts). Genesis event has seq=1.

ts.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp. Informational only; ordering is by seq. Cross-machine clock skew is tolerated.

kind.description: Actor class. 'human': end user typing into a terminal. 'agent': AI agent driving the CLI. 'system': kernel/shell-emitted events with no external actor. 'automation': CI runner, scheduled task, git hook, daemon.

id.description: Non-empty actor identifier. Shape is shell-defined (email, agent id, hostname, etc.); the kernel only enforces non-empty.

session_id.description: Session identifier (CLAUDE_SESSION_ID, CURSOR_TRACE_ID, or CAWS-minted capsule id). Optional. The kernel does NOT verify session liveness or agents.json membership; that's a shell-layer concern.

platform.description: Platform identifier (e.g. 'claude-code', 'cursor', 'github-actions'). Optional. Used for display formatting like '<sessionId>:<platform>'.

actor.description: Required structured actor. Identity is part of the evidentiary claim every event makes. The kernel enforces shape; the shell decides who the actor is.

event.description: Closed event vocabulary. Each event type has a corresponding schema under events/<event>.v1.json describing its data payload and spec_id requirement. Types without a payload schema accept any object.

spec_id.description: Spec identifier when the event is spec-scoped. Required for events in REQUIRES_SPEC_ID class; forbidden for events in NO_SPEC_ID class. The kernel's REQUIRES_SPEC_ID/OPTIONAL_SPEC_ID/NO_SPEC_ID constants are the runtime authority. When absent, the field is omitted from the JSON entirely (NOT serialized as null).

data.description: Per-event-type payload. Validated against the event's specific schema (events/<event>.v1.json) when one exists; otherwise accepts any object. Every event must carry a data field even when empty (use {}).

prev_hash.description: Hash of the previous event's event_hash. null for the genesis event (seq=1). Format when set: 'sha256:' + 64 lowercase hex chars.

event_hash.description: Hash of canonicalJson(this event minus event_hash) prefixed with the domain separator 'caws.events.v1' + 0x00. Algorithm authority is the kernel evidence module, not this schema.

$comment:
Event vocabulary classes (authority: packages/caws-kernel/src/evidence/types.ts):

REQUIRES_SPEC_ID: spec_created, spec_validated, spec_updated, spec_activated, spec_closed, spec_archived, spec_archive_pruned, spec_retired, spec_restored, spec_deleted, spec_scope_amended, spec_drift_detected, spec_binding_cleared, evidence_recorded, ac_recorded, test_recorded, gate_evaluated, waiver_applied, waiver_revoked, worktree_bound, worktree_ownership_seized

OPTIONAL_SPEC_ID: worktree_created, worktree_merged, worktree_destroyed, worktree_pruned, worktree_untracked, claim_taken_over, commit_made

NO_SPEC_ID: session_started, session_ended, branch_switched, doctor_completed, chain_rotated

The class is not encoded in this envelope schema directly because JSON Schema's allOf/if chains for 24 event types would be unreadable; the kernel encodes the classes as TypeScript Sets and validates at append time.
